subject: GRB 220522A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 02:39:00 UT on 22 May 2022, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 220522A (trigger 674879945.37741 / 220522110).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 32.6, Dec = 4.0 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 02h 10m, 4d 00'), with a statistical uncertainty of 10.7 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 37.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220522110/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn220522110.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220522110/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn220522110.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220522110/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn220522110.gif
